articles.title: Wembanyama Drops 41 and 24 to Stun Thunder in 2OT Classic
articles.summary: Victor Wembanyama put up 41 points and 24 rebounds to carry the San Antonio Spurs past the Oklahoma City Thunder 122-115 in double overtime, stealing Game 1 of the Western Conference Finals on the road. The performance made Wembanyama the youngest player in NBA history to record a 40-point, 20-rebound game in the playoffs. It happened on the very night Shai Gilgeous-Alexander received the MVP trophy in the same building.
articles.content:
Some nights in sports feel like they belong in a movie script. Monday night at Paycom Center in Oklahoma City was one of them. Victor Wembanyama walked into the arena, watched Shai Gilgeous-Alexander pick up his second MVP award, then went out and played what may be the greatest playoff game of his young career.

The San Antonio Spurs won 122-115 in double overtime, taking a 1-0 lead in the Western Conference Finals. Wembanyama finished with 41 points, 24 rebounds, three assists, and three blocks in 49 minutes of play. That is not a typo. Forty-one and twenty-four. In double overtime. On the road.

The game itself was a rollercoaster. The Spurs jumped out to a 12-3 lead and were up by 10 late in regulation, only for Oklahoma City to claw all the way back. The Thunder went ahead by three late in the first overtime, but Wembanyama refused to let the Spurs die. He pulled up from 28 feet near the OKC logo on the floor and buried a shot to tie the game at 108-108 and force a second overtime. In that second extra period, Wembanyama outscored the entire Thunder team 9-7 on his own, finishing the game with a thundering and-one dunk over Chet Holmgren.

Rookie Dylan Harper was sensational too, dropping 24 points alongside a team playoff-record seven steals. But this night belonged to Wembanyama. He joined Wilt Chamberlain as the only players in history to record 40-plus points and 20-plus rebounds in a Conference Finals debut. He also became, at 22 years and 134 days old, the youngest player in NBA history to hit that 40-20 mark in any playoff game. After the final buzzer, Spurs CEO R.C. Buford walked past a barefooted Wembanyama and said simply, "Unbelievable, man." Hard to argue with that. Game 2 is Wednesday, back in Oklahoma City.
keyPoints: Wembanyama posted 41 points and 24 rebounds in 49 minutes of double overtime play
The Spurs won 122-115 to go 1-0 up in the Western Conference Finals
Wembanyama hit a 28-foot shot to tie the game late in the first overtime and force a second OT
The game happened on the same night Gilgeous-Alexander received his second MVP award
Dylan Harper set a Spurs playoff record with seven steals in the same game

articles.title: Jury Tosses Musk's OpenAI Lawsuit: Too Late to Sue, They Said
articles.summary: A federal jury in Oakland, California unanimously rejected Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman, finding that Musk had waited too long to bring the case. The jury reached its verdict in under two hours after 11 days of testimony, and the decision was immediately adopted by District Court Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers. Musk has vowed to appeal, calling the outcome a "calendar technicality."
articles.content:
Elon Musk sued OpenAI and its CEO Sam Altman in February 2024, claiming they had betrayed the company's founding mission as a nonprofit and unjustly enriched themselves when OpenAI shifted to a for-profit structure. He had co-founded OpenAI back in 2015 alongside Altman and others, contributing $38 million in its early years, before leaving the board in 2018. The trial that followed became one of the most watched legal battles in Silicon Valley history.

On Monday, it ended with a swift and decisive loss for Musk. A nine-member advisory jury deliberated for less than two hours before unanimously finding that Musk had filed his lawsuit outside of the three-year statute of limitations. The jury did not rule on whether Musk's underlying claims had any merit at all. The case was thrown out entirely on timing grounds. Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers, of the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California, immediately adopted the jury's findings as her own.

The stakes had been enormous. Musk's legal team had sought to force OpenAI and Microsoft to return as much as $180 billion in what they called "ill-gotten gains," remove Altman and OpenAI president Greg Brockman from their leadership roles, and unwind the corporate restructuring that made OpenAI one of the most valuable private companies in the world, currently valued at $852 billion. The jury's verdict means none of that happens.

OpenAI's attorney William Savitt called the verdict confirmation that the lawsuit was "a hypocritical attempt to sabotage a competitor." OpenAI's lawyers had argued throughout the trial that Musk himself had pushed for a for-profit structure at various points, even suggesting OpenAI merge with Tesla, and that he only turned against the company after failing to gain control of it. They pointed to the fact that Musk launched his own AI company, xAI, in 2023, framing the lawsuit as competitive warfare dressed up as principle.

Musk fired back on X, calling Judge Gonzalez Rogers a "terrible activist" judge and writing that the court "handed out a free license to loot charities." His lead attorney Marc Toberoff confirmed an appeal is coming. The trial itself produced a stream of revelations, including texts between Musk and Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg discussing a possible joint bid to buy OpenAI, and details about Musk's long-running romantic relationship with Shivon Zilis, who had served as a back-channel between Musk and OpenAI after he left the board.
keyPoints: A federal jury unanimously rejected Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman after less than two hours of deliberation
The case was dismissed on statute of limitations grounds, not on the merits of Musk's claims
Musk had sought up to $180 billion in damages and the removal of Altman and Greg Brockman
OpenAI argued Musk supported a for-profit structure but sued only after losing control of the company
Musk vowed to appeal, calling the verdict a "calendar technicality"

articles.title: Enzo Maresca Verbally Agrees to Replace Pep Guardiola at Man City
articles.summary: Enzo Maresca has reached a verbal agreement to become Manchester City's next manager, succeeding Pep Guardiola who is expected to leave after the 2025-26 season. Transfer reporter Fabrizio Romano confirmed the deal, with Maresca set to sign an initial three-year contract at the Etihad Stadium. The appointment ends Guardiola's extraordinary 10-year reign, during which he guided City to 16 major trophies including the Premier League, Champions League, and FA Cup.
articles.content:
The Pep Guardiola era at Manchester City is over, and his replacement is already lined up. Fabrizio Romano has confirmed that Enzo Maresca has reached a total verbal agreement to become Manchester City's next manager, with the 46-year-old Italian set to sign an initial three-year deal at the Etihad Stadium.

Guardiola departs as arguably the greatest manager of his generation. Since arriving from Bayern Munich in 2016, he guided Manchester City to 16 major trophies, including six Premier League titles, four of them in a row from the 2020-21 to 2023-24 seasons. That four-in-a-row feat was the first time any club had managed it in Premier League history. His final league game in charge is expected to be Sunday's match against Aston Villa.

Maresca is not a stranger to the club or its methods. He previously served as one of Guardiola's assistants during City's historic treble-winning 2022-23 season, in which the club claimed the Premier League, Champions League, and FA Cup all at once. He then took his own managerial career forward by winning the Championship with Leicester City in 2024 before joining Chelsea, where he won the UEFA Conference League and the FIFA Club World Cup in 2025. His time at Chelsea ended early in January 2026 following internal tensions with ownership at Stamford Bridge.

Guardiola himself has publicly backed Maresca as a manager, saying: "One of the best managers in the world, Enzo Maresca, I know him quite well, but the job he has done at Chelsea does not get enough credit." Sky Sports reports that Manchester City see Maresca as a natural successor specifically because of his deep familiarity with the club's structure, dressing room culture, and tactical system. He plays the same possession-heavy, build-from-the-back football that made City so dominant under Guardiola, which is exactly what the club wants to preserve.

The pressure on Maresca will be enormous. Following the most successful manager in Premier League history, at a club whose fanbase now expects titles as a baseline, is as hard as it gets in football. But his résumé is strong, Guardiola trusts him, and Manchester City are clearly betting that keeping the philosophy intact is the fastest path back to the top.
keyPoints: Fabrizio Romano confirmed Enzo Maresca has verbally agreed to a three-year deal as Manchester City manager
Pep Guardiola is leaving after 10 years and 16 major trophies, including 6 Premier League titles
Maresca previously served as Guardiola's assistant during City's treble-winning 2022-23 season
He won the UEFA Conference League and FIFA Club World Cup with Chelsea before leaving in January 2026
Guardiola publicly praised Maresca as 'one of the best managers in the world'

articles.title: Sports Illustrated Deletes Writer Parker Loverich After AI Plagiarism Accusation
articles.summary: Sports Illustrated scrubbed every article by writer Parker Loverich from its site after Sportico editor Dan Bernstein accused the outlet of using AI to plagiarize his original reporting. The deleted article had lifted data from a Sportico investigation into prediction market Kalshi without any attribution. Sports Illustrated owner Minute Media blamed an independent publisher and said it cut ties with them, but the damage to the brand's already battered reputation is real.
articles.content:
After a writer was accused of using AI to plagiarize a Sportico article about prediction market Kalshi, Sports Illustrated deleted the writer's profile from its site and appears to have cleared out recent content from its prediction market vertical. This is not a good look. It is especially not a good look for a publication that has been here before.

Here is what actually happened. On May 13, Sportico's Dan Bernstein and Lev Akabas wrote about how users have lost more than $100 million placing parlay bets from Kalshi's retail app and website in 2026 so far. On May 15, Parker Loverich published a piece on Sports Illustrated titled "Who is really winning on Kalshi parlays according to the data," which essentially regurgitated the same information and analysis as the Sportico article but failed to offer any attribution. On May 17, Bernstein called out Sports Illustrated on X, accusing the outlet of "stealing entire stories from people without credit, seemingly using AI." Within about 90 minutes, Sports Illustrated took down the offending article. Then it deleted Loverich's entire profile and all his articles too.

Speaking to Futurism, Bernstein said it was "frustrating" to see his work plagiarized by a high-trafficked brand. "If someone was plagiarizing my work and no one was actually seeing it, then it would be semi-annoying," he said. "But the idea of a plagiarized version showing up in Google over my own version, or someone seeing the plagiarized version and then citing the plagiarized version instead of my own, that's kind of frustrating."

A Sports Illustrated spokesperson told Awful Announcing: "Parker Loverich is a real reporter. The predictions market On SI site was managed by an independent publisher who is expected to abide by Sports Illustrated's editorial guidelines. Sports Illustrated became aware of a violation of those guidelines in regards to the use of AI and immediately took steps internally to address this violation, including cutting ties with the publisher."

Back in 2023, Futurism published an investigation revealing that Sports Illustrated, then owned by The Arena Group, had published a huge number of product review articles bylined by completely fabricated writers with AI-generated profile pictures. Following that reporting, several Arena Group executives were fired including the company's then-CEO, and The Arena Group soon lost control of Sports Illustrated entirely. The historic brand ended up in the portfolio of Minute Media. New owner, same story.

Bernstein added that "it would be nice" if Minute Media "took some sort of responsibility," and warned that "just because this account was deleted, I would not be surprised if this is happening elsewhere, or if this is going to happen again." "It feels like it's more of a systemic problem than this one incident," he said.
keyPoints: Sports Illustrated deleted all articles by writer Parker Loverich after an AI plagiarism accusation
Sportico's Dan Bernstein accused Sports Illustrated of using AI to steal his original Kalshi parlay data story
The disputed article was published May 15 and deleted within 90 minutes of Bernstein's public callout on May 17
Sports Illustrated owner Minute Media blamed an independent publisher and said it cut ties with them
This is the second major AI scandal for Sports Illustrated, following the fake AI-generated writers controversy in 2023

articles.title: Gucci and Alpine F1 in Talks for a Title Sponsorship Deal
articles.summary: Gucci is in talks with the Alpine F1 team over a sponsorship deal that could put the Kering-owned fashion house on Alpine's racing cars, potentially from the 2027 season. The talks are being driven in part by Luca de Meo, who became Kering's CEO in 2025 and previously used F1 to grow Alpine's brand when he ran Renault. No deal has been confirmed, but Alpine's current title sponsor BWT is expected to depart at the end of 2026, leaving a major slot open.
articles.content:
Fashion and Formula 1 have been getting closer for years, but this one is big. The Alpine Formula One team is currently in discussions with Gucci regarding a potential sponsorship agreement that would feature the Kering-owned luxury brand on the team's racing cars. Alpine is exploring partnerships with several brands, including Guccio Gucci SPA, though negotiations remain ongoing and no final decision has been reached.

The person connecting these two worlds is Luca de Meo. De Meo was appointed as Kering's chief executive in 2025. He previously served as the head of Renault, which holds majority ownership of Alpine, and during his tenure at Renault he was instrumental in using F1 to elevate Alpine's global profile. He knows exactly how this sport works as a marketing machine, and now he is sitting in the chair at Gucci's parent company.

The deal would see the Enstone-based outfit race under the name "Gucci Alpine Formula One Team" and the sponsorship could be worth between 30 and 60 million euros annually. That is serious money. The potential Gucci deal arrives as Alpine's current title partnership with BWT, known for its pink and blue livery, is scheduled to conclude at the end of this season. Reports suggest Alpine could adopt Gucci's traditional red, green and gold colour palette in place of the pink livery. That would be a stunning visual transformation.

After the Alpine F1 team achieved a fourth-place finish in 2022 and attracted a $900 million valuation following investments from Ryan Reynolds and RedBird Capital Partners in 2023, it faced several difficult seasons. The team finished bottom of the constructors' standings in 2025 with just 22 points, but has made a stronger start to 2026 and currently sits fifth. The switch to Mercedes power this season has delivered better results.

Tommy Hilfiger currently partners with the Cadillac team, New Era has a deal with Williams, and Adidas works with both Audi and Mercedes. Gucci joining would take the fashion-in-F1 trend to a whole new level. Alpine driver Pierre Gasly already serves as an ambassador for Givenchy and Lacoste, giving Alpine an existing foothold in the luxury fashion world that Gucci could build on. The pieces are in place. Now everyone is waiting to see if the deal crosses the finish line.
keyPoints: Gucci and Alpine F1 are in talks over a sponsorship that could be worth 30 to 60 million euros per year
Kering CEO Luca de Meo's history running Renault and growing Alpine through F1 is the key connection
Alpine's current title sponsor BWT is expected to exit at the end of the 2026 season
A Gucci deal could see Alpine race under the name Gucci Alpine Formula One Team from 2027
Alpine driver Pierre Gasly is already a fashion ambassador for Givenchy and Lacoste

articles.title: Meta Moves 7,000 Staff to AI Units Days Before Cutting 8,000 Jobs
articles.summary: Meta is reassigning 7,000 employees to four brand-new AI-focused organisations, just days before laying off roughly 8,000 more workers. The reshuffle, announced via an internal memo from Meta's head of human resources Janelle Gale, is the clearest sign yet of how completely Meta is reorienting itself around artificial intelligence. The company employed more than 78,000 people at the end of 2025.
articles.content:
Meta told employees on Monday that 7,000 of them are moving to new jobs. Meta told employees it was reassigning 7,000 workers to focus on new initiatives around artificial intelligence. Employees will be moved to four new organisations focused on building new AI tools and apps, Janelle Gale, Meta's head of human resources, said in an internal memo. The organisations will use "AI-native design structures" and have fewer managers per employee than other parts of the company, with company leaders set to send details about the new roles on Wednesday.

The timing here is striking. The reassignments were announced before layoffs of roughly 8,000 employees, or 10 percent of Meta's workforce, on Wednesday. Meta, which owns Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp, employed more than 78,000 people at the end of 2025. So in the space of a single week, Meta is simultaneously pulling thousands of people toward AI and cutting thousands of others loose entirely.

The money behind this move is enormous. According to Meta's latest quarterly earnings, Meta now expects 2026 capital expenditures between $125 billion and $145 billion, an increase from prior guidance, driven by higher component costs and the need to expand data centre capacity for future AI demand. That is not a company dabbling in AI. That is a company betting its entire future on it.

Meta has been on this road for a while. Mark Zuckerberg has spoken repeatedly about making Meta an "AI-first" company, and products like Meta AI, built into Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp, have already reached hundreds of millions of users. The reassignment of 7,000 workers is part of a broad corporate restructuring that includes planned staff reductions later this week. The four new AI organisations are where Meta believes the next wave of growth will come from, and it is clearing the decks to get there as fast as possible.
keyPoints: Meta is reassigning 7,000 employees to four new organisations building AI tools and apps
The announcement came via an internal memo from Janelle Gale, Meta's head of human resources
The new organisations will use AI-native design structures with fewer managers per employee
Roughly 8,000 additional employees face layoffs on Wednesday, representing about 10 percent of Meta's workforce
Meta expects to spend between $125 billion and $145 billion in capital expenditure in 2026, largely on AI infrastructure

articles.title: Lanterns Drops New Trailer, Premieres August 16 on HBO
articles.summary: HBO has locked in August 16, 2026 as the premiere date for Lanterns, its DC Studios series starring Kyle Chandler and Aaron Pierre. The new trailer goes bigger than the first teaser, confirming Nathan Fillion returns as Guy Gardner and showing Laura Linney in a mystery role. It also reveals Hal Jordan suiting up and visiting another planet, a sign this show has ambitions beyond a small-town murder mystery.
articles.content:
HBO just gave fans the date they have been waiting for. Lanterns, the DC Studios series built around Green Lanterns Hal Jordan and John Stewart, will premiere on Sunday, August 16, 2026, at 9 PM on HBO and HBO Max. That is the same prestigious Sunday night slot held by House of the Dragon and The Last of Us, which tells you exactly how much HBO is betting on this one.

The new trailer is a big step up from the first teaser, which kept things deliberately grounded. This time, Lanterns leans into the superhero side of things. There is a shot of Kyle Chandler's Hal Jordan fully suited in the classic Green Lantern uniform while visiting what looks like another planet entirely, a clear hint that the show's dark Earth-based murder mystery is just the opening act for something much bigger. The trailer also shows off the Power Ring in action, including a stunning moment where Hal uses it to create a shield against what appears to be an orbital laser.

Two big casting confirmations arrive with this trailer. Nathan Fillion will reprise his role as Guy Gardner, the cocky Green Lantern he played in last year's Superman film. And Oscar winner Laura Linney appears in an undisclosed role, which is the kind of vague credit that tends to mean the character is a major plot point the show wants to protect. The central dynamic between Chandler's weathered veteran Hal and Aaron Pierre's rookie John Stewart is front and center too, and it looks genuinely tense in the best way.

The series was co-created by Chris Mundy of Ozark, Damon Lindelof of Watchmen and The Leftovers, and Eisner Award-winning comics writer Tom King, all three of whom co-wrote the pilot. Mundy serves as showrunner, with the first two episodes directed by James Hawes. The supporting cast is stacked: Kelly Macdonald, Garret Dillahunt, Poorna Jagannathan, Jason Ritter, Ulrich Thomsen, J. Alphonse Nicholson, and Jasmine Cephas Jones all appear. August 16 cannot come fast enough.
keyPoints: Lanterns premieres August 16, 2026 on HBO and HBO Max in the coveted Sunday 9 PM slot
Nathan Fillion reprises his Guy Gardner role from the Superman film
Laura Linney joins the cast in a role the show is keeping secret for now
The new trailer confirms the story goes beyond Earth, with Hal Jordan seen on another planet
Co-created by Damon Lindelof, Chris Mundy, and comics writer Tom King

articles.title: Disney Sued for Scanning Guests' Faces at Disneyland Without Real Consent
articles.summary: Disney is facing a class action lawsuit over facial recognition technology it quietly rolled out at Disneyland and California Adventure in April 2026. The lawsuit, filed May 15 in California federal court, seeks at least $5 million and argues that millions of guests including children were scanned without adequate notice or written consent. It is the sharpest legal challenge yet to a growing wave of face-scanning tech spreading across sports and entertainment venues.
articles.content:
Disney introduced facial recognition at the entrances to Disneyland Park and Disney California Adventure in April 2026, pitching it as a faster way to verify tickets. When guests enter the parks, they scan their park ticket under a screen, and if it is the first time the guest has used their ticket, the system scans their face and links it to the ticket. After the first scan, the guest can enter the park with just a facial scan. The technology cuts down on lines since facial scans are quicker than waiting for guests to pull out paper tickets or access them on their phones.

But a lawsuit filed on May 15 says Disney went about this the wrong way. The suit seeks at least $5 million in damages and alleges that millions of guests including children were scanned without adequate notice or written consent. Disney says captured facial data is deleted within 30 days, but the lawsuit disputes that claim, arguing the images persist indefinitely inside the ticket and pass photo database, making the 30-day deletion policy effectively meaningless.

Disney's defence is that it put up signs. Disney placed signs featuring a silhouette with a slash through it at entrances, offering an opt-out. Plaintiffs' attorney Blake Yagman says a small sign is nowhere near good enough. "Guests should be able to expressly opt in to this type of sensitive facial recognition technology with written consent — the onus of privacy rights should not be on the victim," Yagman wrote in the complaint.

This case is bigger than one theme park. The lawsuit challenges a trend spreading across major sports and entertainment venues of using facial recognition to prevent fraud, heighten safety, and facilitate purchases. Sports stadiums use it to streamline entry, theme parks use it to manage crowd flow, and some musicians rely on it to identify stalkers. But mass adoption of the technology has sparked concern over the specter of a privatized surveillance state and the commercialization of sensitive personal information. Madison Square Garden uses it to ban entry for the "enemies" of its owner, James Dolan.

Disney is no stranger to data privacy battles. The filing comes after Disney last year paid $10 million to settle a complaint brought by the Federal Trade Commission over the collection of children's data on YouTube videos. The proposed class action claims the facial recognition system violates California consumer protection and privacy laws and could affect millions of visitors, including children. The outcome of this case could set the rules for face-scanning at entertainment venues across the country.
keyPoints: Disney rolled out facial recognition at Disneyland and California Adventure in April 2026 to speed up ticket verification
A class action lawsuit filed May 15 seeks at least $5 million and alleges guests were scanned without proper consent
The lawsuit disputes Disney's claim that facial data is deleted within 30 days
Plaintiffs argue guests should have to actively opt in with written consent rather than hunt for an opt-out sign
The case is part of a wider legal pushback against face-scanning tech spreading across sports and entertainment venues

articles.title: Refn's Her Private Hell Stuns Cannes With 7-Minute Ovation After Director Reveals He Died
articles.summary: Nicolas Winding Refn returned to Cannes after a 10-year absence with Her Private Hell, a neon-drenched horror thriller starring Sophie Thatcher and Charles Melton, and the crowd gave it a seven-minute standing ovation. After the credits rolled, Refn stunned the room by revealing he had died for 25 minutes and had to be resuscitated, and that the film grew directly from that experience. Her Private Hell opens in US theaters on July 24, 2026.
articles.content:
Nicolas Winding Refn has always known how to make an entrance. The Danish filmmaker behind Drive and The Neon Demon showed up at Cannes 2026 with Her Private Hell, his first feature film in 10 years, and walked away with one of the festival's biggest ovations. Seven minutes of applause, to be exact. Lead actress Sophie Thatcher burst into tears as the credits rolled, while Refn paced back and forth on stage, visibly hyping the crowd.

Then he said something that stopped the room cold. Refn told the audience that a few years ago he had died for 25 minutes and had to be resuscitated following severe heart failure. Her Private Hell, he explained, was born from that experience. The film is his way of processing what happened and returning to cinema with something deeply personal. For a director known for cool detachment and controlled neon-soaked visuals, it was a surprisingly raw moment.

The film itself is a horror thriller set in a futuristic metropolis. Sophie Thatcher, best known from Yellowjackets and Companion, plays Elle, a young woman searching for her father after a mysterious mist descends on the city and unleashes a deadly entity called Leather Man, who is going around killing women. Her path collides with Charles Melton's American GI, who is on his own desperate mission to rescue his daughter from Hell. Melton is the Riverdale actor who reignited his career at Cannes in 2023 when Todd Haynes' May December premiered here, and he is back in the same city doing it again. The supporting cast includes Havana Rose Liu, Kristine Froseth, Diego Calva, and Hidetoshi Nishijima. The score was composed by Italian legend Pino Donaggio, famous for his work with Brian De Palma and Dario Argento.

Refn's history at Cannes is complicated and that is exactly why people love him there. He won Best Director in 2011 for Drive. Then Only God Forgives in 2013 and The Neon Demon in 2016 split audiences right down the middle, with The Neon Demon triggering boos and walkouts. Her Private Hell screened Out of Competition so it is not in the running for the Palme d'Or, but that ovation suggests the crowd was very much on board this time. Neon is releasing Her Private Hell in US theaters on July 24, 2026, across 800 to 1,200 screens.
keyPoints: Her Private Hell earned a seven-minute standing ovation at Cannes 2026
Refn revealed he died for 25 minutes and had to be resuscitated, which inspired the film
Sophie Thatcher stars as Elle, searching for her father in a deadly, mist-covered future city
The film features a killer called Leather Man and a supporting cast including Charles Melton and Diego Calva
Her Private Hell opens in US theaters on July 24, 2026

articles.title: Banksy's $18M 'Mona Lisa' Goes to Auction in New York Wednesday
articles.summary: A never-before-seen Banksy work titled Girl and Balloon on Found Landscape is expected to break records when it goes under the hammer at an invitation-only sale in New York on May 20. Critics have called it Banksy's "Mona Lisa," and the piece carries an estimated value of $18 million. The secretive sale adds another layer of mystique to a work that has never been seen publicly before.
articles.content:
Banksy is about to make auction history. A never-before-seen work titled Girl and Balloon on Found Landscape is heading to a glitzy, invitation-only sale in New York on May 20, and experts believe it could fetch around $18 million. If that estimate holds, it would set a new record for the anonymous British street artist, whose identity remains one of the art world's most famous unsolved mysteries.

Critics have already started calling Girl and Balloon on Found Landscape Banksy's "Mona Lisa," which is a bold comparison but not a random one. The Girl with Balloon image is probably Banksy's single most recognizable motif, a simple silhouette of a child reaching toward a red heart-shaped balloon. It has appeared on walls, in galleries, and on merchandise worldwide for over two decades. The fact that this version has never been seen publicly before makes it exceptionally rare, even by Banksy's famously secretive standards.

The invitation-only format of the sale is itself a statement. Banksy built his entire career on making art accessible, plastering unauthorized works on public walls in cities around the world as a direct rejection of the exclusive gallery system. An $18 million piece sold behind closed doors to a select list of wealthy collectors sits in obvious tension with that ethos. It is the kind of contradiction that tends to follow Banksy everywhere, and it is part of what makes this auction so interesting to watch.

Banksy's auction history is already full of memorable moments. The most famous came in 2018 when a version of Girl with Balloon partially shredded itself through a hidden mechanism the moment it was sold at Sotheby's in London. That piece, later renamed Love is in the Bin, subsequently resold in 2021 for around $25 million, proving that Banksy's stunts only make his work more valuable. Girl and Balloon on Found Landscape arrives without any confirmed theatrics attached, but with Banksy, you never quite know until the hammer falls.
keyPoints: Banksy's Girl and Balloon on Found Landscape is estimated to sell for $18 million at auction on May 20
The work has never been publicly seen before, making it exceptionally rare
Critics are calling it Banksy's "Mona Lisa" due to the iconic Girl with Balloon motif
The sale is invitation-only, held in New York City
Banksy's 2018 shredding stunt turned a Girl with Balloon piece into a $25 million artwork when it resold in 2021

articles.title: Gracie Cochrane Exits HBO Harry Potter, Ginny Weasley Recast for Season 2
articles.summary: Child actor Gracie Cochrane, who plays Ginny Weasley in HBO's upcoming Harry Potter series, is stepping away from the role after Season 1 due to what her family described as "unforeseen circumstances." The news lands just as Season 1 wraps production and prepares for a Christmas 2026 premiere. The timing is especially significant because Ginny has a central, much bigger role in Season 2, which adapts Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets.
articles.content:
HBO's Harry Potter series has not even aired its first episode yet, and it is already navigating its first major cast change. Gracie Cochrane, the young actress cast as Ginny Weasley, will not be returning for Season 2. Her family confirmed the news in a joint statement, citing "unforeseen circumstances" as the reason for her exit.

The statement from Cochrane and her family was warm but clear. "Due to unforeseen circumstances Gracie has made the challenging decision to step away from her role as Ginny Weasley in the HBO Harry Potter series after Season 1," they said. They added that her time in the Harry Potter world "has been truly wonderful" and that Gracie "is very excited about the opportunities her future holds." HBO responded supportively, saying they are grateful for her work and wish her and her family the best.

Here is why the timing matters so much. Ginny Weasley barely appears in the first book, Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone, which Season 1 is adapting. She shows up briefly at King's Cross station and not much more. But in Season 2, which covers Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets, Ginny is absolutely central to the plot. She is the one tricked into opening the Chamber and unleashing a murderous giant serpent inside Hogwarts. Recasting now, before Season 2 begins production, means the new actress will carry one of the heaviest storylines of the whole series.

Season 1 of Harry Potter is set to premiere on HBO this Christmas 2026, and the show has already been officially renewed for Season 2, which is currently in pre-production at Leavesden Studios outside London. A search for the new Ginny Weasley is now underway. Given that the original cast was chosen from tens of thousands of submissions, expect the search for her replacement to be just as intense.
keyPoints: Gracie Cochrane is leaving the HBO Harry Potter series after Season 1 due to 'unforeseen circumstances'
HBO will recast the role of Ginny Weasley for Season 2 and beyond
Ginny has a massive, central role in Season 2, which adapts The Chamber of Secrets
Season 1 of Harry Potter premieres on HBO at Christmas 2026
This is the first major recast in the HBO Harry Potter series

articles.title: Everlane Sells to Shein for $100M: Ethical Fashion's Sad End
articles.summary: Everlane, once the poster child for transparent and ethical fashion, is reportedly being sold to Shein for $100 million. The deal is a stunning fall from grace for a brand that built its entire identity on calling out the fashion industry's worst habits. That this buyer is Shein, widely considered the least ethical major fashion brand in the world, makes the irony almost too much to bear.
articles.content:
There was a time when Everlane felt like proof that fashion could be better. The brand launched with a radical promise of "radical transparency" — publishing the true cost of every item it made and positioning itself as the antidote to fast fashion's exploitation. For a certain generation of millennial shoppers, buying an Everlane sweatshirt felt like a small act of conscience.

That story is now officially over. Everlane is reportedly being sold to Shein for $100 million, according to Fast Company. Shein is the ultra-fast-fashion giant that has faced years of accusations over labor abuses, intellectual property theft, and an environmental footprint that critics describe as catastrophic. If you were designing the most symbolic possible buyer to bury Everlane's legacy, Shein would be it.

The sale did not come out of nowhere. Everlane had been on shaky financial ground for years. Majority owner L Catterton, a private equity firm backed in part by LVMH, had been shopping Everlane around for a buyer. The $100 million price tag is a modest sum by fashion industry standards, reflecting just how far the brand had slipped from its peak hype days in the mid-2010s when it had a waitlist culture and a devoted following.

The deal stings because Everlane was not just a clothing brand. It was a cultural signal. Buying Everlane meant you cared, or at least wanted to look like you did. Now that signal has been absorbed by Shein, a company that represents everything Everlane claimed to stand against. For anyone who bought into the millennial-era dream that ethical consumption could change the fashion industry from within, this one hurts.
keyPoints: Everlane is reportedly being sold to Shein for $100 million
Everlane built its brand around ethical sourcing and radical price transparency
Shein is widely regarded as one of the least ethical fashion companies in the world
Majority owner L Catterton had been shopping Everlane around after years of financial difficulty
The deal marks a symbolic end to the millennial ethical-fashion movement

articles.title: ChatGPT Is Turning C Students Into A Students, Berkeley Study Finds
articles.summary: A University of California, Berkeley study analysing over 500,000 course grades found that A grades in writing and coding classes jumped by about 30 percent after ChatGPT launched in late 2022. The lead researcher, Igor Chirikov, put it bluntly: we now have C students who are A students. The findings are alarming employers and pushing elite universities including Harvard and Yale to rethink how grading even works.
articles.content:
Getting straight As used to mean something. According to a new University of California, Berkeley study, it may not anymore. The research, led by Igor Chirikov, a senior researcher at Berkeley's Center for Studies in Higher Education, analysed more than 500,000 course grades from 2018 to 2025 at a large public university in Texas. The conclusion is pretty stark: in classes where AI tools like ChatGPT are most useful, the share of A grades has shot up by about 30 percent since ChatGPT's debut in late 2022.

The classes hit hardest are the ones you would expect. Writing-heavy and coding-heavy subjects, the kind where a student can hand a prompt to ChatGPT and get a polished essay or working code back in seconds, saw the biggest grade jumps. Classes where AI cannot help as much, like sculpture or lab-based courses, saw grades stay basically flat over the same period. Chirikov told Axios the data is not showing A-minus students nudging up to an A. "We have a C student who is now an A student," he said.

The researchers describe three ways students use AI: augmentation, where it supports their own work; reinstatement, where AI introduces new kinds of tasks; and displacement, where it simply does the work for them. Only the first two actually build skills. The third just inflates the grade. And take-home assignments, the staple of modern university life, are the perfect environment for displacement to happen without anyone noticing.

The ripple effects are already being felt well beyond campus. Harvard College published a report in February arguing that current grading practices make it impossible for employers to compare students fairly, and faculty are currently voting on a proposal to cap the proportion of A grades awarded. Yale said in April that grades at peer institutions "no longer" communicate what students have learned. Meanwhile employers are caught in a bind, discouraging AI-generated application materials while also expecting candidates to be AI-proficient.

Some educators are already adapting. At Wharton, Chelsea Schein, who researches hiring trends at Veris Insights, has cut the weight of take-home assignments and shifted toward in-class assessments where AI use is restricted. The broader lesson from the Berkeley research is that if universities do not change how they assess students, a generation could graduate with the grades but not the skills, creating a workforce that depends on AI for the very tasks they were supposedly trained to do.
keyPoints: A Berkeley study of over 500,000 grades found A grades rose by around 30 percent in AI-exposed classes after ChatGPT launched
Researcher Igor Chirikov described the shift as turning C students into A students
Take-home assignments are the biggest vulnerability because AI can complete them without detection
Harvard is voting on capping A grades, and Yale has said its grades no longer communicate what students have learned
Employers face a paradox: they want AI-savvy hires but do not want AI-written applications

articles.title: Netflix Streams Its First Ever Live F1 Race This Weekend
articles.summary: Netflix is airing a live Formula 1 race for the first time ever, streaming the 2026 Canadian Grand Prix from May 22 to 24 for US subscribers. The race is part of a content-swap deal with Apple TV, which now holds exclusive US broadcasting rights for the full F1 season. If you already have Netflix and want to dip into live F1 without signing up for Apple TV, this weekend is your only window.
articles.content:
Something big is happening on Netflix this weekend and it has nothing to do with a new drama series. The 2026 Canadian Grand Prix, running May 22 to 24 in Montreal, will be available to Netflix subscribers in the United States, marking Netflix's first live F1 broadcast. Yes, actual live racing, not a documentary. Lights out and they are going.

Apple TV holds the full US F1 season from 2026 under a five-year agreement worth roughly $750 million, or about $150 million per year. That is nearly double what ESPN, which held the US rights for eight years, paid at around $85 to $90 million annually. ESPN is now completely out of the picture.

As part of the same arrangement, Apple and Netflix exchanged content. Netflix gets to air one race, the Canadian Grand Prix, while Apple TV gains US streaming rights for Formula 1: Drive to Survive Season 8. Netflix subscribers can stream the full race weekend, including practice, qualifying, and the Grand Prix itself on May 24, live on the platform. It is a genuine two-platform moment for F1 in the US.

The Canadian Grand Prix slot looks like a deliberate test. Netflix has previously broadcast live sports including MLB games, and the logic here is clear: millions of Drive to Survive viewers have never watched a race live. One high-profile broadcast is a low-risk way to find out how many of them will. Whether Netflix pushes for more races down the line is the question everyone in the sport is asking.
keyPoints: Netflix is live-streaming the F1 Canadian Grand Prix for the first time ever, May 22 to 24
The race is at Circuit Gilles Villeneuve in Montreal, with the main race on May 24 at 2:50 PM ET
Apple TV holds full US F1 rights in a deal worth around $750 million over five years
Netflix got the Canadian GP slot as part of a content swap that gives Apple TV access to Drive to Survive Season 8
This is only available to US Netflix subscribers — UK viewers still watch on Sky Sports

articles.title: Amazon Alexa Podcasts Now Makes Custom AI Shows in Minutes
articles.summary: Amazon has launched Alexa Podcasts, a new feature inside Alexa+ that generates on-demand podcast episodes on virtually any topic using AI. The feature draws from licensing deals with more than 200 news outlets, including the Associated Press, Reuters, and the Washington Post. Episodes are narrated by two AI-generated co-hosts and can be ready in minutes.
articles.content:
Amazon just changed how you might get your daily news fix. The company launched Alexa Podcasts on May 18, 2026, a feature built into Alexa+ that lets you generate a full podcast episode on basically any topic you can think of. Sports recap? Ancient Roman history? The state of the music industry? Just ask, and Alexa builds it for you.

Alexa Podcasts lets users ask Alexa+ to create a podcast on any topic, and the system researches the subject, produces a structured overview, and delivers it as an audio episode narrated by two AI-generated co-hosts in a conversational format. Amazon says Alexa will pull together the relevant information, give you an overview of what it plans to cover, and let you adjust the length and direction conversationally before generating anything. Once you are happy with the plan, Alexa creates a recording with AI-generated host voices.

To make sure the content is actually reliable, Amazon went big on media partnerships. Amazon has signed licensing agreements with more than 200 news organisations, including the Associated Press, Reuters, the Washington Post, Time, Forbes, Business Insider, Politico, USA Today, Condé Nast, Hearst, and Vox Media, along with more than 200 local newspapers across the United States. The local newspaper angle is especially interesting. Local news organisations in the United States have been in financial crisis for more than a decade, with thousands of newsrooms closing as advertising revenue migrated to digital platforms. A licensing deal with Amazon provides immediate revenue, but it also risks accelerating the displacement of the audience relationship that local publishers depend on for subscriptions, donations, and community engagement.

Amazon is not the first to try this. Google's NotebookLM, Spotify's AI-generated playlists, and Apple's personalised news digests all reflect the same thesis: that users prefer AI-curated content over the effort of selecting sources themselves. But Amazon has one massive advantage over all of them. Amazon's integration of the feature into Alexa+, which is bundled free with Prime, means that the barrier to trying it is effectively zero for the more than 200 million Prime members worldwide. Amazon also says it is already working on new forms of AI-generated audio content, including personalized news briefings and custom reports based on personal documents.
keyPoints: Amazon launched Alexa Podcasts on May 18, 2026, generating custom podcast episodes on any topic in minutes
Episodes feature two AI-generated co-hosts and draw from over 200 licensed news outlets including AP, Reuters, and the Washington Post
The feature is free for Amazon Prime members via Alexa+, or $19.99 per month for non-Prime users
Amazon plans to expand into personalized news briefings and podcasts built from users' own uploaded documents
The move raises questions about traffic and revenue impact on the news publishers whose content powers the feature

articles.title: All Things Go NYC 2026: Brandi Carlile, MUNA, Zara Larsson Headline
articles.summary: All Things Go NYC has revealed its 2026 lineup, with Brandi Carlile, MUNA, Zara Larsson, and Lola Young headlining three nights at Forest Hills Stadium in Queens from September 25 to 27. Carly Rae Jepsen also joins as a special sunset performance exclusive to the New York edition. Presale tickets go on sale May 20 starting at $99 for a single day.
articles.content:
The festival fans affectionately call "lesbian Coachella" is back with one of its biggest New York lineups yet. All Things Go NYC 2026 will take over Forest Hills Stadium in Queens from September 25 to 27, and the bill is entirely made up of female and non-binary artists, a tradition that makes All Things Go genuinely unlike any other festival on the calendar.

Headlining the three days are Brandi Carlile, MUNA, Zara Larsson, and Lola Young. This is a milestone moment for Zara Larsson specifically. It marks her first-ever headline festival set anywhere, a long-awaited payoff for an artist whose mainstream breakthrough kept feeling like it was just around the corner. MUNA are practically All Things Go royalty at this point, and they made their feelings clear in a statement: "Extremely stoked to be headlining one of our favorite festivals to play in a city that means so much to this band. Let's go, lesbians!"

The biggest New York exclusive on the poster is Carly Rae Jepsen, slotted in for a special sunset performance. Jepsen is not playing the All Things Go DC edition at Merriweather Post Pavilion, so if you want to see her this festival season, Queens is your only shot. Also on the bill are Rebecca Black, Cara Delevingne, The Beaches, Hemlocke Springs, CMAT, Jensen McRae, Sienna Spiro, Grace Ives, Blu DeTiger, Meg Stalter, Chloe Qisha, Natalie Jinju, and Cherry Bomb.

One of the most emotionally charged storylines heading into the weekend involves Lola Young. Last year at All Things Go NYC, Young collapsed on stage during her set, an incident that led her to cancel her North American tour and step back from performing before returning to the stage in early 2026. She addressed her comeback directly: "All Things Go, we have unfinished business. I feel blessed and ready to take it to the next level. Thank you for having me back."

All Things Go is now a three-city operation in 2026. The DC edition at Merriweather Post Pavilion features Hayley Williams and Mitski, while the Toronto edition at RBC Amphitheatre brings Lorde and Kesha. The NYC version has always had its own distinct identity, and this year's all-star lineup cements it as the jewel of the three. Presale tickets open May 20 at 10 AM ET, with general sale starting May 21. Single-day passes start at $99 and three-day passes at $225.
keyPoints: All Things Go NYC runs September 25 to 27 at Forest Hills Stadium in Queens
Brandi Carlile, MUNA, Zara Larsson, and Lola Young headline the all-female and non-binary lineup
Carly Rae Jepsen performs a special sunset set exclusive to the NYC edition
Lola Young makes an emotional return after collapsing on stage at All Things Go NYC last year
Presale opens May 20 at 10 AM ET; single-day tickets start at $99

articles.title: Malta Gives Every Resident Free ChatGPT Plus for a Year
articles.summary: Malta has become the first country in the world to partner with OpenAI and hand every resident a free year of ChatGPT Plus. The catch is a good one: residents must first complete a free AI literacy course built by the University of Malta. It is the boldest national AI adoption move any government has made so far.
articles.content:
OpenAI and the Government of Malta announced a world-first deal that gives every Maltese citizen and resident free access to ChatGPT Plus for one full year. OpenAI signed the deal to provide all Maltese citizens and residents with free ChatGPT Plus for one year, with one condition: they must first complete an AI literacy course developed by the University of Malta. The programme is called AI for All, and its first phase launched in May 2026.

Citizens and residents registered with Malta's online identity system can apply after completing the free online AI for All course. According to the Malta Digital Innovation Authority, the course is designed to help people understand what AI is, what it can and cannot do, and how to use it responsibly at home and at work. All citizens and residents aged 14 and over will have free access to the course and will receive an official certificate upon completion.

The initiative is designed to ensure that Malta's approximately 574,250 residents do not fall behind in the digital age. The offer even extends to Maltese citizens living abroad. Malta's Economy Minister Silvio Schembri put it plainly: "Malta is the first country to launch a partnership of this scale because we refuse to let our citizens stay behind in the digital age."

This is not just a nice story about a small island nation. It is a signal of where things are heading. Last year Anthropic gave all teachers in Iceland free access to Claude, and in September 2025 OpenAI announced a partnership with the Greek government to bring its technology to secondary schools and start-ups. Malta just leapfrogged all of them. The partnership is the latest under the OpenAI for Countries initiative, OpenAI's work to support governments as they move from early AI interest to strategic national adoption. Other countries are watching closely.
keyPoints: Malta is the first country to offer free ChatGPT Plus to every resident through a national government deal with OpenAI
Residents must complete the AI for All course from the University of Malta before claiming their free year of access
The Malta Digital Innovation Authority is managing the rollout and distribution of subscriptions
The offer covers Maltese citizens living abroad as well as residents on the island
The partnership sits under OpenAI's broader OpenAI for Countries programme aimed at national AI adoption

articles.title: Gaten Matarazzo Makes West End Debut as Mark Cohen in Rent Revival
articles.summary: Stranger Things star Gaten Matarazzo will play Mark Cohen in a new West End revival of Rent, opening at the Duke of York's Theatre in London on September 26, 2026. The production marks the 30th anniversary of Jonathan Larson's Pulitzer Prize and Tony Award-winning rock musical, and Matarazzo's first major London stage role. Tickets went on sale today with 10,000 seats priced at £35 and under.
articles.content:
Gaten Matarazzo has been playing Dustin Henderson on Stranger Things since he was a kid, but theatre is actually where he started. And now he is making his West End debut in one of the most beloved musicals ever written. Matarazzo will star as Mark Cohen in a new revival of Rent, opening at the Duke of York's Theatre in London on September 26, 2026, with an official opening night on October 8.

Rent is Jonathan Larson's rock musical about a group of young artists living in New York's East Village in the early 1990s, navigating love, ambition, poverty, and the AIDS crisis. It is loosely inspired by Puccini's opera La Bohème and features songs like Seasons of Love, One Song Glory, and La Vie Bohème. Larson won the Pulitzer Prize for Drama and the Tony Award for Best Musical for the show, but tragically died of an aortic dissection the night before its Off Broadway opening in 1996. The last time Rent played London's West End was at the Shaftesbury Theatre in 1998, where it ran for 18 months.

Matarazzo is described by producer Chris Harper as a self-described "Rent-head," which tracks. He is no stranger to the stage, having played Tobias in the acclaimed Broadway revival of Sweeney Todd at the Lunt-Fontanne Theatre and racking up credits in Dear Evan Hansen and Parade. Mark Cohen is the aspiring filmmaker at the heart of the story, the narrator who documents his friends' lives as everything around them falls apart. It is a role that suits someone who can hold the emotional centre of a sprawling ensemble.

Directing is Luke Sheppard, who recently won the Olivier Award for Best Director of a Musical for Paddington The Musical. This West End production is based on Sheppard's 2020 staging at the 120-seat Hope Mill Theatre in Manchester, which Harper saw just before Covid-era rules shut theatres down. It took six years of navigating complicated rights and getting approval from Jonathan Larson's estate to finally bring that production to London. Producers Harper and Sonia Friedman are teaming for the first time here. The run is booked through February 13, 2027, and the producers have committed 10,000 tickets at £35 and under to keep it accessible.
keyPoints: Gaten Matarazzo makes his West End debut playing Mark Cohen in Rent
Rent opens at the Duke of York's Theatre on September 26, 2026 for its 30th anniversary
The show last played London's West End in 1998 at the Shaftesbury Theatre
Director Luke Sheppard just won the Olivier Award for Best Director for Paddington The Musical
10,000 tickets are available at £35 and under

articles.title: Aqua, the "Barbie Girl" Group, Breaks Up After 30 Years
articles.summary: Aqua, the Danish-Norwegian eurodance group behind the 1997 global smash "Barbie Girl," has announced their breakup after more than three decades together. Members Lene Nystrøm, René Dif, and Søren Rasted made the announcement on Instagram, saying now feels like "the right moment to say goodbye." The group sold an estimated 33 million records across their career and found a massive new audience in 2023 when "Barbie Girl" was sampled for the Barbie movie soundtrack.
articles.content:
It is the end of a plastic-fantastic era. Aqua, the Danish-Norwegian eurodance group who gave the world "Barbie Girl," have officially called it quits after more than 30 years together. Members Lene Nystrøm, René Dif, and Søren Rasted broke the news on Instagram, bringing the curtain down on one of the most recognisable pop acts of the 1990s.

In their farewell post, the group reflected on what those decades meant to them. "AQUA has been such a huge part of our lives, and together we've had the chance to experience more than we ever dared to dream of," they wrote. They said that after being together this long, "you also learn when it's time to protect what you've created together," and that now feels like "the right moment to say goodbye, while the memories are still strong."

Aqua formed in 1995 and broke through in a massive way with their 1997 debut album Aquarium, which contained "Barbie Girl." That song topped charts across the globe and became one of the most recognisable pop songs ever recorded. It also landed the band in legal trouble when toy giant Mattel sued them over the lyrics, though the case was eventually dismissed after a judge found the song was a fair parody. The group went on to release Aquarius in 2000 and Megalomania in 2011, racking up an estimated 33 million records and singles sold over their career.

This is not actually the first time Aqua has stepped away. The band initially broke up in 2001, then reunited in 2007 for another run. Original member Claus Norreen had already departed the group back in 2016, leaving the trio of Nystrøm, Dif, and Rasted to carry the name forward. This time, it sounds final.

The group got a huge second wind as recently as 2023 when Nicki Minaj and Ice Spice sampled "Barbie Girl" for their track "Barbie World" on the blockbuster Barbie movie soundtrack. That moment introduced "Barbie Girl" to an entirely new generation, making this goodbye feel especially bittersweet. For now, René Dif is already looking ahead, with a solo DJ set booked in Stockholm next month.
keyPoints: Aqua, best known for the 1997 hit "Barbie Girl," have officially announced their breakup
Members Lene Nystrøm, René Dif, and Søren Rasted shared the news on Instagram
The group sold an estimated 33 million records across their three-decade career
Aqua had previously broken up in 2001 before reuniting in 2007
"Barbie Girl" got a major revival in 2023 via a Nicki Minaj and Ice Spice sample on the Barbie movie soundtrack

articles.title: Kendall Jenner and Jacob Elordi Double Date with Kylie and Timothée
articles.summary: Kendall Jenner and Jacob Elordi have been spotted on a double date alongside Kylie Jenner and Timothée Chalamet, confirming the new couple is going fully public. Kendall and the Euphoria star were first romantically linked after Coachella 2026. The double date signals the relationship is getting serious fast.
articles.content:
Kendall Jenner and Jacob Elordi are not hiding anything. The pair, who were first romantically linked after Coachella 2026, have now been spotted on a double date with Kylie Jenner and Timothée Chalamet, making this one of the most talked-about celebrity foursomes in recent memory.

The Jenner sisters and their respective partners stepping out together is a big deal. Kylie and Timothée Chalamet have been one of Hollywood's most scrutinised couples, and Kendall bringing Jacob Elordi into that same social circle sends a clear signal. This is not a quiet, low-key thing anymore.

Jacob Elordi is best known for his role as Nate Jacobs in HBO's Euphoria, the wildly popular drama series that made him one of the most recognisable faces of his generation. Standing at 6'5" with a string of major roles including Elvis Presley in Priscilla, Elordi has become a genuine A-list name. Pairing him with one of the most-followed people on the planet in Kendall Jenner makes this a tabloid supernova.

For fans of both families, seeing the Jenner sisters aligned with two of Hollywood's biggest young stars at the same time is a cultural moment in itself. Coachella has a long history of sparking celebrity relationships, and this one looks like it has legs well beyond the festival season.
keyPoints: Kendall Jenner and Jacob Elordi went on a double date with Kylie Jenner and Timothée Chalamet
Kendall and Elordi were first linked romantically after Coachella 2026
The outing marks the new couple going fully public in a high-profile way
Elordi is widely known for his role as Nate Jacobs in HBO's Euphoria

articles.title: Emoji-Suited American Arrested for Breaking Into Punch the Monkey's Enclosure
articles.summary: Reid Jahnai Daysun, 24, dressed in a blue emoji suit and jumped into the macaque enclosure at Ichikawa City Zoo in Japan on Sunday, terrorising around 50 monkeys including viral internet star Punch. Daysun and his companion Neal Jabahri Duan, 27, who filmed the stunt, were arrested by Chiba police on Monday and charged with forcible obstruction of business. The emoji costume is thought to reference a cryptocurrency mascot, and the pair initially tried to lie to police about their names.
articles.content:
Punch the monkey has charmed the entire internet. The nine-month-old Japanese macaque at Ichikawa City Zoo became a global sensation earlier this year after being rejected by his mother at birth and finding comfort in a stuffed orangutan toy gifted by a zookeeper. The little guy carried it everywhere, and the world fell in love. So naturally, someone decided to ruin it.

On Sunday 17 May, Reid Jahnai Daysun, 24, allegedly climbed over a fence and dropped into the macaque enclosure at Ichikawa City Zoo, near Tokyo. He was wearing a full blue suit with a smiling emoji head, a costume thought to reference a Memecoin cryptocurrency mascot. His companion, Neal Jabahri Duan, 27, stood outside and filmed the whole thing. Daysun also reportedly dropped a small stuffed toy near the animals as some kind of stunt.

The roughly 50 macaques living in the enclosure, including Punch, were terrified. They all ran to a rock formation on the far side of the enclosure to get away. A zookeeper arrived shortly after and physically grabbed Daysun by the arm and escorted him out. Chiba police detained both men on Monday. To make things worse, the duo reportedly had no formal ID on them and initially tried to give police false names.

Daysun and Duan were charged with forcible obstruction of business. Both men denied the charges and were set to face further questioning by prosecutors on Tuesday. Ichikawa City Zoo posted on X confirming the pair had been handed over to police, and stated that no abnormalities were observed in any of the animals. The zoo is now installing intrusion prevention nets and setting up permanent patrol units around the enclosure. Punch's fame, it turns out, comes with a serious downside.
keyPoints: Reid Jahnai Daysun, 24, jumped into Punch the monkey's enclosure at Ichikawa City Zoo on Sunday 17 May wearing an emoji costume
Neal Jabahri Duan, 27, filmed the stunt from outside the enclosure
The emoji costume is believed to reference a Memecoin cryptocurrency mascot
All 50 monkeys in the enclosure, including Punch, were unharmed but visibly frightened
Both men were arrested, charged with forcible obstruction of business, and initially lied to police about their identities
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Japanese macaque Ichikawa City Zoo Punch monkey

articles.title: Louvre Names Selldorf and Studios Architecture for Mona Lisa Overhaul
articles.summary: The Louvre has selected Selldorf Architects and Studios Architecture Paris to lead its massive "Louvre New Renaissance" expansion, which will include a brand-new dedicated room for the Mona Lisa. The project will add a second entrance to the museum and increase its capacity by three million visitors a year. The announcement comes after months of delay caused by a $100 million crown jewel heist, staff strikes, and a ticket fraud scandal.
articles.content:
The world's most visited museum just took a major step toward its biggest reinvention in decades. The Louvre announced this week that it has chosen Selldorf Architects and Studios Architecture Paris to design its ambitious "Louvre New Renaissance" expansion, ending a long and chaotic selection process that had been derailed by a string of crises.

The centrepiece of the plan is a custom-built, 33,000-square-foot room dedicated entirely to Leonardo da Vinci's Mona Lisa. Right now, the painting sits in a crowded gallery where visitors jostle for a glimpse from behind a barrier. The new space will let people see the Mona Lisa on its own terms, with room to breathe and detailed information about the painting's history on display. The project will also add a grand second entrance on the museum's eastern side, using the 17th-century Grande Colonnade facade originally built under King Louis XIV, to ease the severe bottlenecking at I.M. Pei's iconic glass pyramid.

Selldorf Architects is led by German-born architect Annabelle Selldorf and is known for museum work including expansions of the Frick Collection in New York and the Sainsbury Wing of the National Gallery in London. Studios Architecture Paris, the French branch of a firm originally founded in San Francisco in 1985, collaborated with the late Frank Gehry on the Fondation Louis Vuitton museum in Paris. The two firms were chosen from a shortlist of five, announced by French Culture Minister Catherine Pégard.

The Louvre currently pulls in around nine million visitors a year, well more than double what the existing pyramid entrance was designed to handle when it opened in 1989. The expansion is President Emmanuel Macron's signature cultural legacy project, though it has been plagued by controversy. The original cost estimate from former museum director Laurence des Cars was around 270 million euros, but France's national auditing authority put the real figure closer to 1.1 billion euros.

The road to this announcement was anything but smooth. The selection was supposed to happen months earlier, but was halted after thieves broke into the Louvre last October and made off with French crown jewels worth more than $100 million. A water leak, unsound structural beams, a ticket fraud scheme suspected of costing the museum up to 10 million euros, and repeated staff strikes all followed. Despite all of that, Louvre's new director Christophe Leribault has confirmed the project is moving forward, with a consultation period between the museum, the winning architects, and stakeholders now beginning.
keyPoints: Selldorf Architects and Studios Architecture Paris will design the Louvre New Renaissance expansion
A new 33,000-square-foot dedicated room for the Mona Lisa will be built
A second entrance using the historic Grande Colonnade will add capacity for 3 million more visitors per year
Cost estimates range from 270 million euros to over 1.1 billion euros
The project was delayed by a $100 million crown jewel heist, strikes, and a ticket fraud scandal

articles.title: Joby Aviation Eyes LIRR Strike as Its New York Breakthrough
articles.summary: The Long Island Rail Road strike stranded nearly 300,000 daily commuters starting May 16, and flying taxi companies like Joby Aviation saw the chaos as their perfect opening. Joby had just completed New York City's first-ever point-to-point electric air taxi demonstration flights in late April, putting it in the right place at the right time. The strike, the first on the LIRR in over 30 years, ended with a deal on Monday night, but the moment gave eVTOL companies a very public argument for why the city needs a sky-based backup plan.
articles.content:
New York got a taste of transportation chaos this past weekend, and flying taxi companies were watching very closely. The Long Island Rail Road, which is North America's largest commuter rail system, went on strike at 12:01 a.m. on Saturday, May 16. Five unions representing around 3,500 workers walked off the job after failing to agree on wages and healthcare costs with the Metropolitan Transportation Authority. The result was immediate misery for the roughly 275,000 people who rely on the LIRR every single weekday.

Joby Aviation had already put itself front and center in New York just weeks earlier. On April 27, Joby completed the first-ever point-to-point electric vertical takeoff and landing, known as an eVTOL, demonstration flights in New York City history, flying routes from JFK Airport to heliports at West 30th Street and East 34th Street in Midtown in under 10 minutes. The company's pitch is simple: turn a 60 to 120-minute drive to JFK into a flight that takes around seven minutes. With the LIRR shut and roads gridlocked, that pitch suddenly felt a lot more urgent.

The strike itself was a political firestorm. New York Governor Kathy Hochul pleaded publicly with unions to return to the table, calling the railroad "the lifeblood of Long Island." New York State Comptroller Thomas DiNapoli estimated the strike cost up to $61 million in lost economic activity every single day it ran. The MTA rolled out 275 shuttle buses, but with over 250,000 daily riders that was nowhere near enough. Teachers like Katie Dolgow, who commutes from Long Island to Manhattan, described burning hours just getting to Queens, let alone the city.

The strike ended with a deal announced Monday night, and limited LIRR service resumed Tuesday at noon. But the episode handed Joby Aviation and rivals like Archer Aviation a ready-made argument they could never have scripted themselves. Joby is in the final stages of FAA certification and has partnerships with Delta Air Lines and Uber to create a seamless ground-to-air travel experience. The company estimates a Manhattan-to-JFK air taxi trip would cost around $200, similar to an Uber Black ride. The LIRR strike just showed New York exactly what happens when the ground gives out.
keyPoints: The Long Island Rail Road strike began May 16 and stranded around 275,000 daily commuters for three days
Joby Aviation had completed New York City's first eVTOL air taxi demo flights from JFK on April 27, just weeks before the strike
The strike cost New York up to $61 million in lost economic activity per day, according to the state comptroller
A deal was reached Monday night and limited LIRR service resumed Tuesday at noon
Joby Aviation is targeting a roughly $200 price point for a JFK to Manhattan air taxi ride, comparable to Uber Black

articles.title: Pabst Kills Schlitz Beer After 177 Years
articles.summary: Pabst Brewing Co. has officially discontinued Schlitz, the Milwaukee-born lager once known as 'the beer that made Milwaukee famous,' ending a 177-year run. Pabst cited rising storage and shipping costs as the reason, saying sales had fallen too low to justify minimum order quantities. Wisconsin Brewing Co. will brew one final 80-barrel farewell batch on May 23, with a send-off party and pickups starting June 27.
articles.content:
Schlitz is done. Pabst Brewing Co. announced on May 15, 2026 that it is discontinuing Schlitz, the American lager that dates all the way back to 1849. The reason is blunt: sales had fallen so low that Pabst could no longer justify the minimum order quantities needed to keep producing it. As Pabst head of brand strategy Zac Nadile put it, the company had to "make the tough choice to place Schlitz Premium on hiatus" due to rising costs to store and ship the product.

It is hard to overstate how big Schlitz once was. The Joseph Schlitz Brewing Company launched in Milwaukee in 1849 and by 1902 had become the best-selling beer in the world, surpassing even Pabst itself. At its peak in the 1950s and 1960s, Schlitz was the number-one beer in America. The brand was so woven into Milwaukee's identity that Sean McCarthy, co-owner of Milwaukee's Wolski's Tavern, compared losing it to losing Harley-Davidson from the city.

The fall from the top was dramatic. In the 1970s, Schlitz management cut corners on the brewing process to save money, which led to flat beer. An attempt to fix it with a stabilization enzyme backfired badly, creating hazy pints with visible floaters. Consumer trust collapsed almost overnight. By 1982, Schlitz was sold to Stroh Brewery Company for $500 million, having lost more than 90 percent of its brand value in under a decade. Pabst eventually acquired the brand in 1999 and relaunched it in 2008 using the classic 1960s formula, but it never regained its national footprint.

At least Schlitz is getting a proper send-off. Wisconsin Brewing Co. brewmaster Kirby Nelson is brewing one final 80-barrel batch on May 23 in Verona, Wisconsin, using a composite recipe built primarily from 1948 brewing logs, the year Schlitz was the best-selling beer in the world. Pre-orders open that same day on Wisconsin Brewing Co.'s website, with a farewell tapping party on June 27. Nelson put it simply: "Things change, but Schlitz deserves better than just to be swept under the rug."
keyPoints: Pabst Brewing Co. officially discontinued Schlitz on May 15, 2026 after 177 years
Rising storage and shipping costs, plus declining sales, were cited as the reasons
Schlitz was once the best-selling beer in the world, peaking in the 1950s and 1960s
A 1970s recipe change and PR disaster destroyed consumer trust and triggered the brand's long decline
Wisconsin Brewing Co. will brew one final 80-barrel batch on May 23, with a send-off party on June 27

articles.title: Disney+ and Hulu to Livestream Bonnaroo, Lollapalooza and ACL Globally
articles.summary: Disney+ is teaming up with Hulu to bring live coverage of three of America's biggest music festivals to a global audience. The partnership covers Bonnaroo, Lollapalooza, and Austin City Limits, streaming across all three events in 2026. It is the first time Disney+ has joined Hulu's annual festival livestream slate, making the shows more accessible than ever before.
articles.content:
Big festival season just got a serious upgrade. Disney+ is joining forces with Hulu to livestream three of the most iconic American music festivals to audiences around the world. We are talking about Bonnaroo, Lollapalooza, and Austin City Limits, all live, all on your screen, no tent or wristband required.

Here is the schedule to lock in. Bonnaroo streams June 11 to 14. Lollapalooza follows July 30 to August 2. Then Austin City Limits closes things out October 2 to 4. That is essentially the entire summer and fall festival calendar wrapped up in one streaming partnership.

Hulu has been the home of festival livestreams for a while now, but bringing Disney+ into the fold is a real step up. Disney+ has a genuinely massive global footprint, which means fans outside the US who have never been able to watch these festivals live now have a real shot at tuning in. It turns a very American tradition into a worldwide event.

If you have ever wanted to catch a headlining set without battling heat, mud, or overpriced food, this is your moment. Keep an eye on both Disney+ and Hulu closer to each festival date for full lineup and streaming details.
keyPoints: Disney+ is joining Hulu to co-stream Bonnaroo, Lollapalooza, and Austin City Limits in 2026
Bonnaroo streams June 11–14, Lollapalooza July 30–August 2, Austin City Limits October 2–4
It is the first time Disney+ has been part of Hulu's festival livestream coverage
The global reach of Disney+ means international fans can now watch these US festivals live
No ticket needed — just a Disney+ or Hulu subscription

articles.title: AI Turns 50-Year-Old Erotic Magazines Into Films at Cannes
articles.summary: A collection of AI-generated short films built from erotic magazine photo spreads published 50 years ago has debuted on the sidelines of the Cannes Film Festival. The project is streaming now on Cultpix, with a physical release planned on both BluRay and VHS from Klubb Super 8. It is one of the most provocative examples yet of AI being used to animate vintage analogue media into new moving image content.
articles.content:
The Cannes Film Festival has always been a place where boundaries get tested, and this year an AI project is doing exactly that from the fringes of the main event. A collection of AI-generated short films sourced from erotic magazine photo spreads originally published around 50 years ago has made its debut on the sidelines of Cannes 2026, streaming on the specialist platform Cultpix.

The project takes analogue print media, the kind of glossy erotic magazines that lined newsagent shelves in the mid-1970s, and uses artificial intelligence to bring those still images to life as short films. The physical release is being handled by Klubb Super 8, available on both BluRay and VHS. That VHS choice is not accidental. It deliberately mirrors the format that originally brought adult content into living rooms decades ago, and it gives the whole project a very specific retro texture.

What makes this culturally interesting is not just the adult content angle. It is what the project says about where AI image and video generation has arrived. Tools that can animate static photography into believable moving footage are now sophisticated enough to be premiered at one of the world's most prestigious film festivals, even if this particular screening is happening in the margins rather than on the Palais des Festivals main screen. Cannes has long used its sidelines as a space for exactly this kind of provocateur work.

Cultpix, the streaming home for this release, specialises in cult and arthouse cinema, which positions this project as a knowing piece of film history as much as a technology demonstration. The pairing of AI generation with 1970s source material raises genuine questions about authorship, archival ethics, and what counts as a new creative work. Those debates are only going to get louder as the tools improve and more archives become fair game for AI treatment.
keyPoints: AI-generated short films built from 1970s erotic magazine photo spreads debuted at Cannes 2026
The project is streaming on Cultpix with a physical release on BluRay and VHS from Klubb Super 8
The VHS format release echoes the medium that first brought adult content into homes in the 1970s
The project sits on the sidelines of Cannes rather than in official competition
The work raises questions about AI authorship and the ethics of animating archival material
